generated_oml:
// C3: Physical sensing components
instance CurrentSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Measures the input electrical current of each robot motor."
    DTDFVocab:producedData CurrentDataTransmitted
]

instance MotorVelocitySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Measures motor angular velocity for each joint."
    DTDFVocab:producedData VelocityDataTransmitted
]

instance AxisPositionSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Measures the first‑axis linear position of the manipulator."
    DTDFVocab:producedData AxisPosDataTransmitted
]

instance JointTorqueSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Torque sensor mounted on each rotary joint."
    DTDFVocab:producedData TorqueDataTransmitted
]

instance JointTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Temperature sensor monitoring joint temperature."
    DTDFVocab:producedData TemperatureDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction (DataTransmitted)
instance CurrentDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CurrentSensor
]

instance VelocityDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorVelocitySensor
]

instance AxisPosDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom AxisPositionSensor
]

instance TorqueDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom JointTorqueSensor
]

instance TemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom JointTemperatureSensor
]

// C10: Data
instance CurrentData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw input‑current trace from motor sensors."
    DTDFVocab:fromData CurrentDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PreprocessEnabler
]

instance VelocityData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw angular‑velocity trace from motor sensors."
    DTDFVocab:fromData VelocityDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PreprocessEnabler
]

instance AxisPosData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw first‑axis position trace."
    DTDFVocab:fromData AxisPosDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PreprocessEnabler
]

instance TorqueData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw joint‑torque trace from torque sensors."
    DTDFVocab:fromData TorqueDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PreprocessEnabler
]

instance TemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw joint‑temperature trace from temperature sensors."
    DTDFVocab:fromData TemperatureDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PreprocessEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers (Processing components)
instance PreprocessEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Applies a low‑pass filter to incoming sensor traces and prepares reference data."
    DTDFVocab:enables MQTTEnabler
]

instance MQTTEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Publishes each pre‑processed data point as a JSON‑encoded message on MQTT topics as defined by the AAS."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataRepositoryService
]

instance AASExecutorEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Executes the Asset Administration Shell logic, assembling the digital shadow in Thingsboard."
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictiveMaintenanceService
    DTDFVocab:enables MachiningParameterOptimizationService
]

instance SimulinkFrictionEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Runs the Simulink white‑box model of joint friction and kinematics."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeFrictionIdentificationService
]

instance NeuralNetRegressorEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Executes the black‑box neural‑network regressor that predicts friction characteristics from incoming traces."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeFrictionIdentificationService
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeFrictionIdentificationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuously updates physics‑based and data‑driven friction models and generates compensation commands."
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionCompensationAction
]

instance PredictiveMaintenanceService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Monitors anomalies in friction behaviour to detect early faults."
    DTDFVocab:provides AnomalyDetectionInsight
]

instance MachiningParameterOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Simulates and optimizes machining parameters (feed rate, spindle speed) before production."
    DTDFVocab:provides MachiningParameterOptimizationInsight
]

instance DataRepositoryService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Stores measured, simulated and reference values in the Thingsboard digital shadow."
    DTDFVocab:provides DataArchivingAction
]

// C17: Insights / Actions
instance FrictionCompensationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatically injects friction‑compensation commands into the robot control loop."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance AnomalyDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Provides early‑fault detection insight based on deviation of observed friction from model predictions."
]

instance MachiningParameterOptimizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Recommends optimal feed rate and spindle speed settings for the current workpiece and load."
]

instance DataArchivingAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatically archives raw and processed data in the central Thingsboard repository."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The digital twin operates on a real‑time time scale. Sensor data from the robot are streamed continuously over MQTT to the digital shadow, and the friction simulation models are executed such that the resulting updates are delivered within the allowable dead‑time of the robot’s control loop, enabling tight synchronization between the physical robot and its digital counterpart."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The system is orchestrated as a pipeline: live robot data are published via MQTT, received by an Asset Administration Shell (AAS) executor which populates measured, simulated and reference values into a digital shadow stored in ThingsBoard; the shadow’s structure is defined by a SysML model while the behavior (friction dynamics) is provided by executable Simulink simulation models integrated through the Functional Mock‑up Interface (FMI). A visualization dashboard pulls the consolidated data for monitoring and analysis. This arrangement connects the physical robot, communication middleware, AAS, storage, modeling artifacts and user interface into a coordinated digital twin constellation."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The DT lifecycle follows the MBSE abstraction chain described in Document 1: (i) requirements model, (ii) functional architecture model, (iii) logical architecture model, and (iv) product/technical layer model. After these engineering models are instantiated, the Digital Shadow reference model (Document 2) adds a data acquisition stage (automated one‑way flow from the physical object), a processing‑component stage that consumes input data traces, applies engineering, simulation or data‑calculation models, and produces output traces, and a traceability stage that links each output trace back to its generating component, input traces and models via the ‘originates‑from’ association. Transition between stages is governed by the conformance relation that maps concrete elements to reference‑model concepts, providing lifecycle governance."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Model fidelity is addressed by linking the DT to measured data traces and simulated data traces; the Digital Shadow Reference Model mandates traceability of each data trace to its source models via the originates‑from relation, supporting verification of transformation correctness (e.g., SysML → simulation models). The approach stresses that the reliability of the DT depends on the correctness of underlying models and data transformations, and therefore requires traceability, verification of model transformations, and validation against measured data. Uncertainty sources are implicitly managed through this traceability, enabling error detection by comparing simulated outputs with measured traces and correction by updating model parameters."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The implementation relies on the MQTT publish‑subscribe protocol over TCP/IP for communication between the preprocessing component, the broker, and downstream consumers. MQTT topics are organized hierarchically and can be assigned read/write permissions. Messages are untyped and may contain numeric values or JSON payloads. The Asset Administration Shell provides the mapping of system sub‑models to MQTT topics, facilitating integration of sensor data into the digital shadow. No specific bandwidth, latency, or redundancy mechanisms are described."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "Integration with external systems relies on standardized Industry 4.0 protocols: MQTT is used for IoT sensor data transport, ThingsBoard serves as the IoT data storage and visualization platform, and the Asset Administration Shell (AAS) provides a normative wrapper for exposing the robot’s structure (SysML) and behavior (Simulink/FMI) to other services. These standards enable interoperability with other enterprise IT components and potential additional digital twins."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The documents reference several modelling and communication standards used in Digital Twin engineering: SysML and UML for requirements and architectural models; ACME, AADL, MontiArc, and MontiArc‑Automaton as component‑&‑connector ADLs; DEVS as a discrete‑event simulation formalism; and MathWorks Simulink for continuous‑time simulation (Document 1). Conformance to a reference model is enforced by an explicit conformance relation defined in Document 2, providing a formal compliance framework. For data exchange, the MQTT protocol is mentioned together with its Mosquitto implementation (Document 6), and the open‑source IoT platform ThingsBoard (v4.0.0) is listed as a supporting infrastructure (Document 6). These standards and tools constitute the interoperability and certification basis described in the provided materials."
]
